forecaster_note: Surf Guru read: A long-period S/SSW groundswell is already in the water and building hard through the weekend — 6.2ft @ 9s from 186° today, stretching to 7.2ft @ 11s from 181° by Sunday, with deep-water energy climbing 432 → 706 → 1104 kJ Fri→Sat→Sun. Winds are the gift: light W-to-SW offshore every morning. The catch is direction — near-dead-S (181–186°) wraps poorly into Dee Why and is shadowed at Manly, so the size lives at the S-magnets Long Reef (up to 2x overhead) and Curl Curl. Dee Why still rates fair-good off the small ENE 76° secondary and clean offshore grooming. Best all-rounder is Saturday; biggest is Sunday. ⚠️ SharkSmart detection feed was down this refresh (503) — tap through to the live map before you paddle out.

wind.summary: Light WSW offshore ~15–20 km/h through your mid-morning window — clean and groomed. Swings W tomorrow, SW Sunday, offshore each morning; dawn-to-midday is the play before any arvo sea breeze.

shark.summary: SharkSmart NSW detection feed not available this refresh (server 503) — tap to check live detections before paddling out.

days.label: Today
days.note: Long-period S/SSW groundswell already running under light WSW offshore — clean, but at 186° it wraps soft into Dee Why. The size lives at the S-magnets: Long Reef is head-to-2ft-overhead, Curl Curl chest-to-overhead. Dee Why and Manly stay smaller, though the ENE 76° secondary grooms Dee Why up to a fair-good rating. Mid-morning around the 9:34am low is your window.

dee-why.window: The Point is the play — the ENE 76° component banks cleanest here and the light WSW offshore holds it open. 432 kJ is a fun-size, not a power day; go on the pushing low mid-morning.

tip.label: Frontside tip
tip.text: Dead-S 186° wraps weak into Dee Why, so sit wide on the Point where the ENE 76° secondary corners in. At 432 kJ and 100% consistency it's clean and consistent but not throwing — generate off the bottom turn.

long-reef.window: Biggest of the four — the S swell magnet turns 186° into head-to-2ft-overhead. WSW is more cross-shore here so it'll carry some bump; go earliest for the cleanest faces.

tip.label: Backside tip
tip.text: This is where the size is — head-high-plus sets off a solid S groundswell. Wind's cross so pick the lulls; strong paddle rewarded on the bigger ones. Step up if the sets stack.

curl-curl.window: Second for size behind Long Reef and cleaner banks — solid chest-to-overhead. Mid-morning low is best; cross-WSW so earliest is smoothest.

tip.label: Backside tip
tip.text: More exposed than Dee Why so it grabs the S swell — punchy corners on the low tide. On your backhand stay high through the fatter sections and hunt the banked lefts.

manly.window: Smallest and most protected — waist-to-chest, clean under the WSW offshore. Fine for a mellow one; not worth the drive over Long Reef or Curl Curl.

tip.label: Backside tip
tip.text: North Steyne stays small on a dead-S swell — soft, forgiving corners. Longboard or the groveller if you just want water time; offshore all morning.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: Swell builds and cleans — the S train stretches to 11-second period and energy nearly doubles to ~706 kJ, with W offshore grooming every beach. Long Reef jumps toward 2x overhead; Dee Why finally gets a chest-to-overhead push. Best all-round day of the three — get the mid-morning window.

dee-why.window: Longer 11s period fills in — real size for the first time this week, chest-to-overhead. W offshore all morning; mid-morning on a mid tide.

long-reef.window: Cranking — up to 2x overhead with the W offshore cleaning it up. Standout of the weekend for size plus shape; step-up board and treat it with respect.

curl-curl.window: Clean chest-to-overhead-plus with the W offshore holding the faces open. Best size-plus-shape balance and more manageable than Long Reef.

manly.window: Still the smallest as the swell swings S — mellow but clean under the offshore. The manageable option if the others are too much.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: Peaks Sunday — 1104 kJ and 11s period, the biggest of the run. Still dead-S (181°), so Long Reef and Curl Curl hold the size (up to 2x overhead at the Reef) while Dee Why and Manly stay shadowed. SW offshore each morning; get in before the arvo sea breeze. Sunday is the swell of the week for the S-magnets.

alerts: Near-dead-S swell (181°) — 1104 kJ on paper but it wraps badly into Dee Why. The Point grooms it; the beachies stay well under what the energy suggests.
dee-why.window: SW offshore mornings. The Point is the play — a wrapping S swell can hold a long clean right while the open beach stays shadowed. Rates fair-good off shape, not size.

long-reef.window: Biggest of the run — solid 2ft-overhead sets Sunday at the peak. Cross-SW adds some bump; earliest is cleanest. The serious session of the week — step-up board.

curl-curl.window: Head-high-plus at the Sunday peak, punchy on the low. Takes the S swell far better than Dee Why; cross-SW so go early.

alerts: S swell heavily shadowed by the southern headland — smallest of the four by a wide margin despite the 1104 kJ figure.
manly.window: Clean but shadowed. Only worth it if the bigger spots are too much, or you want an easy paddle.
